Appointment.title: Appointment
Appointment.description: Represents an appointment booking at a LuneCare clinic.

clinicId.description: Reference to the Clinic where the appointment is scheduled. (Relationship: Clinic 1:N Appointment)

patientId.description: Reference to the Patient who booked the appointment. (Relationship: Patient 1:N Appointment)

startTime.description: The date and time the appointment is scheduled to begin.

endTime.description: The date and time the appointment is scheduled to end.

notes.description: Any notes related to the appointment.

Patient.title: Patient
Patient.description: Represents a patient using the LuneCare app.

definition.description: Stores patient profiles.  Uses path-based ownership for data isolation.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

definition.description: Stores appointment bookings for a specific patient. Includes denormalized 'patientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: patientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the patient.

params.name: appointmentId
params.description: The unique identifier for the appointment.

params.name: clinicId
params.description: The unique identifier for the clinic.

firestore.reasoning:
The Firestore structure is designed to ensure data isolation and efficient querying based on user roles and application features.  We leverage path-based ownership for patient data, and a flat collection for Clinics.

Authorization Independence (CRITICAL): Authorization independence is achieved by structuring data such that access control can be determined directly from the document path or the document itself, avoiding the need for `get()` calls to parent documents.  The appointments subcollection includes the patientId. This allows security rules to validate if `request.auth.uid` == `patientId`. By denormalizing in this way, we ensure that access control can be determined independently at the document level.

QAPs Support:  The structure supports secure list operations (QAPs) by segregating data based on ownership. Listing appointments is restricted via the path `/users/{userId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`. This guarantees only a `patient` can list their own appointments, and only a clinic can list appointments at `/clinics/{clinicId}/appointments/{appointmentId}`

Invariants: Ownership is maintained through the path-based approach for patients and their appointments. Timestamps can be added to the documents if needed to track creation or modification times.  The structure will also support any denormalized data required for improved query performance.
